name: Cinnamon Shortbreads
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 170°C (325°F, Gas Mark 3).
2. Grease a 19 cm x 29 cm (7-1/2 inch x 11-1/2 inch) shallow baking tin with butter.
3. Sift together the flours, semolina or ground rice, and cinnamon in a large bowl or food processor. Add any bran residue from the sieve.
4. Add the butter or margarine and caster sugar to the dry ingredients. Mix until a soft dough forms that leaves the sides of the bowl clean.
5. Press the dough into the prepared tin, smoothing the surface with the back of a metal spoon.
6. Prick the entire surface of the shortbread with a fork.
7. Bake for about 45 minutes, or until the shortbread is set and very crispy on top, lightly golden in color.
8. Check doneness by lifting an end piece; if it looks cooked underneath, it's ready. If not, return it to the tin and continue baking.
9. Allow the shortbread to cool and crisp up in the tin before cutting into sections. It can be stored in the tin for several days or frozen for later use.
